title: Bayeux Tapestry arrives in London for forthcoming display at British Museum
subhead: The thousand-year-old, 70-meter-long embroidery depicting William the Conqueror's exploits in medieval England has been removed from its crate after its transfer from France. Staff will begin a thorough check of its condition on Monday, and it will be placed in a custom-made display case a few days later.

title: Elie Barnavi: 'Marwan Barghouti is the only one capable of uniting the Palestinian people around a national project and the two-state solution'
subhead: The former Israeli ambassador to France, in an op-ed for Le Monde, calls for the release of the Palestinian politician, who has been held in Israel since 2002. He argues that Barghouti has consistently championed the project of a sovereign Palestine living in peace alongside Israel.

title: Argentina's thriving theater scene
subhead: Buenos Aires has emerged as the epicenter of Spanish-language theater, where a vibrant creative scene – both precarious and remarkably prolific – is defined by the generosity of its performances.

title: Christophe B., an ordinary man accused of extraordinary violence in 'libertine' rapes
subhead: The 56-year-old is accused of forcing his partners to engage in extreme sexual acts with men he recruited online for over 20 years. He is charged with rapes with torture or acts of barbarity.
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

title: Visualize the full extent of wildfires across Europe in recent weeks
subhead: The burned areas across the European continent have already far surpassed levels seen in recent years and are now approaching the maximum recorded in 2025.

title: How the 2016 Nice terrorist attack unfolded, minute-by-minute
subhead: On July 14, 2016, a terrorist drove a truck into crowds gathered on the famed Promenade des Anglais in the southern French city, killing 86 people. Ten years later, Le Monde looks back at how the tragedy unfolded.
